definition: The credit transmission mechanism is structurally bifurcated: top 5 hyperscalers hold ~$350-400B cash and generate ~$300B+ annual FCF, making 85-90% of AI capex self-funded and rate-insensitive (200bp hike changes WACC by 20-30bp). Simultaneously, fintech's ~25-30% share of consumer lending (up from ~10% in 2019) attenuates the traditional bank lending channel. SLOOS systematically overstates credit tightening as a result.
evidence: 200bp rate hike changes hyperscaler WACC by only 20-30bp
Fintech share of consumer lending ~25-30% (up from ~10% in 2019)
Fed Board research (Jagtiani & Lemieux 2019, Fuster et al. 2022)
open_questions: Does rate insensitivity of the dominant investment channel fundamentally break monetary policy transmission?
Is the fintech credit channel more or less procyclical than traditional bank lending?
How should the Fed calibrate policy when SLOOS systematically overstates tightening?
